race.name: Canadian Birkebeiner
race.tagline: Fifty-five kilometers with a 5.5-kilogram pack through Elk Island's frozen wilderness — Canada's tribute to the warriors who saved a king.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple stations with hot drinks, soup, and snacks

climate.description: February in central Alberta brings deep cold with temperatures routinely dropping below -20°C. The race has been held in conditions as cold as -35°C. The flat prairie geography offers no shelter from Arctic outflow winds. Paradoxically, Chinook winds can push temperatures above freezing within hours.
challenges: Extreme cold — race has been held at -35°C
Mandatory 5.5kg backpack adds physical and logistical challenge
Wind exposure on open prairie sections
Chinook winds can cause rapid temperature swings

course.primary: Point-to-point through Cooking Lake-Blackfoot Provincial Recreation Area and near Elk Island National Park. Rolling terrain through boreal aspen and spruce forest with open prairie sections. The mandatory 5.5kg backpack (representing the baby prince) adds a unique physical challenge.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through boreal forest and prairie
course.grooming: Well-groomed by volunteer crews, Canadian standard
features: Mandatory 5.5kg backpack representing the infant King Haakon
Through or near Elk Island National Park — home to free-roaming bison
Boreal forest and open prairie terrain
One of the coldest marathon ski races in the world

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The mandatory backpack and extreme cold make this race genuinely unique among Worldloppet events. Strong community spirit and cultural significance elevate what would otherwise be a flat prairie race.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 promotes from T3 to T2

history.summary: Founded in 1985 by Edmonton's Norwegian-Canadian community, the Canadian Birkebeiner recreates the 1206 rescue of infant Prince Haakon Haakonsson by two Birkebeiner warriors who skied him to safety across the Norwegian mountains. Like its Norwegian namesake, racers must carry a 5.5kg backpack representing the weight of the baby prince. The race takes place in the Cooking Lake-Blackfoot area east of Edmonton.
notable_facts: Named after the 1206 rescue of infant Prince Haakon by Birkebeiner warriors on skis
Mandatory 5.5kg backpack represents the weight of the baby prince
One of three Birkebeiner races worldwide (Norway, USA, Canada)
Elk Island National Park nearby is home to both plains and wood bison
Has been held in temperatures as cold as -35°C
Worldloppet member event since the series' early years

youtube_data.search_query: Canadian Birkebeiner cross country ski race Edmonton area

videos.title: Edmonton Birkie 2023
videos.description:
Beautiful day skiing The Edmonton Birkie 31km route. Rolling hills and lots of sunshine for a beautiful day. 

Easy check in, shuttle from the host hotel and a warm place to stay until the event started. Amazing feed stations made the event more fun along with the sunshine and beautiful tracks.

A must attend event for all classic xc ski lovers!

videos.title: Canadian Birkebeiner Cross Country Ski Loppet 2013
videos.description:
http://www.TheHowardTeam.net
Bryon Howard is a top performing ReMax Realtor in Calgary, Alberta.  He became intriqued with the Birkenbiner when he first heard about it in 1994. He attempted it for the first time on it's 25 anniversary ... in this video on Feb 9 2013.  

The Birkie is a family-oriented, friendly cross-country ski loppet (recreational event) honouring the spirit of the Norwegian Birkebeiner legend. Reliving an 800-year-old historic legend of the original Birkebeiners who saved the heir to the Norwegian throne in a 55km epic journey. It takes place the second Saturday in February every winter near Edmonton.

It's a premier "loppet" -- a recreational fun citizen race, geared to mass participation, fun and bragging rights for the fastest. Some of Bryon's buddies with Team Mito http://www.mitocanada.org/team/team/nord-skiing/ were the Fastest of the day ... including Jeff Krar who who his age group.

Canada's largest classical-format cross country ski festival.
videos.curation_reason: Personal race experience documenting first-time participation in this family-oriented loppet event.

videos.title: Relive -  Canadian Birkie 2025 from the sky
videos.description:
This amazing video gives an aerial view of the Canadian Birkie Ski Festival, a classical cross-country ski event taking place just 40 minutes east from Edmonton, Alberta. The area, better known as Cooking Lake-Blackfoot Recreational Area lies in the UNESCO-designated Beaver Hills Biosphere. The Canadian Birkie Ski Festival long starts begin at the wonderful Ukrainian Cultural Heritage Village. Martin at Dronebuzz - @MartinWithDroneBuzz filmed the Canadian Birkie 2025 from an aerial perspective. Special Thanks to Dronebuzz.ca and Explore Edmonton  - @exploreedmonton for making this video possible.
#crosscountryskiing #alberta #albertaparks #edmonton #sports

videos.title: Canadian Birkebeiner 2012: World Record attempt for longest ski line
videos.description:
On February 11, 2012 cross-country skiers gathered at the Waskahegan staging area to form the world's longest ski line.

Despite the wind and the cold temperatures there was a great turnout for this event!

videos.title: The Birkie makes a comeback as it turns 40
videos.description:
The Canadian Birkebeiner celebrates 40th anniversary this year with ideal snowfall and sun, after a year off due to no snow.

➤ Subscribe: 
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCn9JnDto9d2-nn4lvJE3Iqw?sub_confirmation=1

➤ Read more on CBC Edmonton: 
https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/edmonton

➤ Read more on CBC Calgary: 
https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/calgary

videos.title: Cross-Country Skiing at the Canadian Birkie Ski Festival 2023
videos.description:
Enjoy this slideshow for a great impression of the 2023 Canadian Birkie Ski Festival. Canada's premier classic Cross Country Skiing event since 1985.

Each year the Canadian Birkebeiner Society organizes the Canadian Birkie Ski Festival in Cooking Lake - Blackfoot Provincial Recreation Area. The aim of the society is to engage participants of all ages and abilities in Cross-Country Skiing as a healthy and active winter activity in beautiful and unique surroundings.

The Canadian Birkie Ski Festival works with JanStudios for it photography. This slideshow was prepared by Jamie and Nadine of https://www.janstudios.com/#!/HOME

videos.title: Cross-Country Skiing in Edmonton
videos.description: Edmonton's expansive river valley and parks system is a perfect cross-country skiing destination. For more information, including a list of trails visit: http://bit.ly/EdmontonCrossCountrySkiing

videos.title: Canadian Ski Marathon celebrates 50th anniversary
videos.description:
The Canadian Ski Marathon is the longest and oldest Nordic ski tour in North America. Over two days, participants ski along the 160 kilometer trail that connects Gatineau and Lachute, Quebec. The route passes through mostly private land, and is thus only publicly accessible and groomed once a year, for the event.

In 2016, the Canadian Ski Marathon is celebrating its 50th anniversary. Pick up the January/February issue of Canadian Geographic for the full story.
